content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the branch diff against `origin/master`, read the adapter/CLI/reconciliation changes, and ran targeted `cargo test` filters for the new health-check and sync paths. I found two fix-now correctness gaps: one in success-reconciliation replay handling and one in the recovered-claim short-circuit path.

## Amendments

1. finding_summary: `reconcile_success()` now blocks idempotent replay even when no new bead mutation is needed. The new preflight at `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:244-246` runs before `close_bead_idempotent()`, but that helper is explicitly read-first and returns early when `br show` says the bead is already closed (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:424-426`). Even if that initial guard were moved, `sync_after_close()` still unconditionally health-gates and runs `sync_flush()` at `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:459-467` instead of using the new `sync_if_dirty()` path. In a crash-recovery replay where the prior close+sync already succeeded, an unrelated later conflict/malformed `.beads/issues.jsonl` now prevents milestone-state completion even though there is nothing left to mutate or flush. severity: medium. affected_files: ["src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs"]. remediation_hint: only gate the branch that will actually call `close_bead`, and use `sync_if_dirty()`/pending-mutation state for replay so already-closed, already-synced recoveries can still advance milestone state without requiring a clean export.
   Rationale: This regresses the crash-safe idempotency that `reconcile_success()` is supposed to preserve. A run can get stuck behind unrelated export damage after the bead work itself is already durably closed, which is exactly the recovery path this code is meant to protect.
2. finding_summary: `claim_bead_in_br()` skips the new health recheck on the recovered-claim short-circuit path. After `sync_if_dirty()` succeeds, lines `src/cli/project.rs:984-989` immediately return `Ok(())` when the recovered flush replayed `update_bead_status(<bead>, "in_progress")`. Unlike the normal explicit-claim path, which rechecks export health before its final sync at `src/cli/project.rs:992-1002`, this early return never verifies that the recovered `br sync --flush-only` left `.beads/issues.jsonl` in a safe state. If the replayed flush itself leaves conflict markers or malformed JSONL behind, the controller records a successful claim and proceeds with a corrupted bead export. severity: medium. affected_files: ["src/cli/project.rs"]. remediation_hint: run the same post-flush health guard before returning from the recovered-claim path, and add a regression test where the replayed `sync --flush-only` both replays the claim and corrupts `.beads/issues.jsonl`.
   Rationale: The change adds health checks specifically to stop operators from proceeding on corrupted bead state, but this branch bypasses that guarantee for the exact retry path it just introduced.
